free_text: Activation cross-sections of longer-lived products of
proton induced nuclear reactions on dysprosium up to
36 MeV
Natural dysprosium foil, thickness: 100.59 microns
A stack containing
natural Dy foils as target, natural Ti foils as
monitor foils and Al foils as energy degraders were
irradiated with a proton beam of 36 MeV, 61nA for
71 minutes. Gamma spectra were measured
non-destructively in 4 series using general cooling
times of 1.5h, 20h, 80h and 330d.
Chapter 4
The uncertainty of the beam energy in the
middle of each irradiated foil was estimated
combining the uncertainties of the energy degradation
depending on the thickness of the target foils in the
stack and the energy straggling. The uncertainty of the
initial beam energy was +-0.3MeV.
The uncertainty of the cross-sections were
estimated taking the independent relative errors of
linearly contributing components, number of the
bombarding particles, number of the target nuclei,
decay data, detector efficiency and peak area were
summed quadratically, and the square root of the sum
was taken. No details on individual error
components are given.
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS, EMPIRE
and ALICE.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
